title: Automation to duplicate subitem to another board but keep in original board
excerpt:
Hi - I have a board I use to track a payment schedule for certain items (the payment schedule is contained in the subitems). 
I have a separate board that is used for any and all requests that come through to me from peo…

posts.author: jenoooo (Jen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi - I have a board I use to track a payment schedule for certain items (the payment schedule is contained in the subitems).

I have a separate board that is used for any and all requests that come through to me from people in my organisation.

I am hoping to find a way to send a sub-item to the Request board when a due date is approaching without completely removing it from the original board.

I have been able to send the item to the request board but it removes the item completely from the original board. I still need the items there and I just cannot figure out if it can be done or not.

Any assistance is appreciated - I am hoping it is an automation I can create rather than using a 3rd party or paid app.

posts.author: jparty (Javier Party)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am wondering why we have the beautiful option to duplicate a subitem manually but it is not possible through automations. I am missing the functionality to:

  • Duplicate subitems
  • Create subitems programmatically
  • Move subitems to another item, to another board

Currently, it is possible to automate the duplication of items and boards, but not subitems. ¿Any news on when it will be doable?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jenoooo & @jparty

We developed an app called subitem automations that provides robust automation capabilities to subitems. It also includes the ability to move subitems between boards, duplicate and more here’s an example →

Screenshot 2024-03-05 at 16.49.332134×432 52.1 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: jparty (Javier Party)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Fantasy-Media-Team. We tried your app and found a bug after the first implementation.

We implemented this automation:

When subitem's STATUS changes to SOMETHING, move subitem to ITEM.

It works just fine, it did the job, but it additionally re-assigns the subitem to the person that already was assigned to that subitem. This triggers an email-notification [You’re assigned], which is far from desirable. In our case, neither the original ITEM, nor the receiving ITEM have an assignee. It would be great if you could either clear up why this is happening or fix it, if possible.

We will keep on trying it for a while. Thank you for your work and for making the subitem automations happen. Anyway, I think this is something that monday.com should actually be able to offer as standard.
